Ali Said is a Python Developer with contact information including email (ali.said@email.com) and phone number (+20 100 123 4567), and links to his GitHub and LinkedIn profiles.
Ali Said received a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science from Savitribai Phule Pune University in June 2018.

Since January 2019, Ali Said has been working as a Python Developer at Atos Syntel, focusing on computer vision for a US-based banking client. His work involves designing and developing computer vision algorithms using OpenCV, PIL, and Numpy, as well as unit testing, debugging, and version control with Git.
In 2018, Ali Said's project idea was selected for the final prototyping round of KPIT-Sparkle and was also submitted to the Accenture Innovation challenge.
In 2017, Ali Said developed and presented a poster on a 'Smart Ambulance' concept, winning First Rank in a college-level competition.
Ali Said has received certificates from ESD Program - Zensar Technologies in 2017 and Employability Training - Barclays, Global Talent Track, & NASSCOM Foundation in 2015.

Ali Said received awards and honors including selection for the final prototyping round of KPIT-Sparkle in 2018, First Rank in a college poster presentation in 2017, a sponsorship of Rs. 15,000 from Platinum Auto in 2017, and participation in the Avishkar Innovation Fair in 2017.
Ali Said possesses skills in Python, Django, REST API, MySQL, HTML5, Git, OpenCV, PIL, and Numpy.

From 2017 to 2018, Ali Said served as Treasurer of the IEEE Student Branch at JSCOE, Pune, managing financial accounts and organizing a workshop on 'Excellence in English and Public Speaking' in 2017.

From 2016 to 2017, Ali Said was a Team Leader for College Events at JSCOE, Pune, leading teams in technical and cultural events and participating in inter-college competitions at SCOE, PICT, and AISSMS.

Ali Said is a driven Python Developer, currently applying his skills in computer vision at Atos Syntel for a US banking client. A 2018 graduate of Savitribai Phule Pune University with a Bachelor’s in Computer Science, he quickly transitioned from academic promise to practical application. His toolkit includes Python, Django, REST APIs, MySQL, HTML5, alongside image processing libraries like OpenCV, PIL, and Numpy – all managed with practiced efficiency using Git. 

Even during his studies, Ali demonstrated a proactive and inventive spirit. A ‘Smart Ambulance’ concept earned him First Rank in a college poster presentation in 2017, and his project ideas garnered recognition from both KPIT-Sparkle and the Accenture Innovation challenge in 2018. He wasn’t solely focused on technical pursuits; as Treasurer of the IEEE Student Branch at JSCOE, Pune, he honed organizational skills, managing finances and even orchestrating a workshop on public speaking. Prior to that, he led teams in college events, navigating the competitive landscape of inter-college competitions. 

Ali’s commitment to continuous learning is evident through certifications from Zensar Technologies and a comprehensive employability training program with Barclays, Global Talent Track, and NASSCOM Foundation. He’s a resourceful individual, once securing a Rs. 15,000 sponsorship from Platinum Auto for a project, and actively participated in the Avishkar Innovation Fair. You can reach him at ali.said@email.com, +20 100 123 4567, or connect with him on GitHub and LinkedIn.

2017 was a year of burgeoning recognition for Ali Said, a period marking a clear ascent in his academic and extracurricular pursuits. It was the year his ‘Smart Ambulance’ concept earned him First Rank in a college poster presentation, a testament to his innovative thinking. This success attracted tangible support, including a Rs. 15,000 sponsorship from Platinum Auto, fueling further development. Beyond the ‘Smart Ambulance’, 2017 saw Ali solidify his skills with certifications from both the ESD Program at Zensar Technologies and an Employability Training program through Barclays, Global Talent Track, and the NASSCOM Foundation – building a foundation for professional life. He wasn’t solely focused on individual achievement; as Treasurer of the IEEE Student Branch at JSCOE, Pune, he demonstrated responsibility and organizational skill, meticulously managing finances and spearheading a workshop focused on communication excellence. His leadership extended back to 2016, continuing into 2017 as a Team Leader for College Events, where he honed his ability to motivate and guide teams through both technical and cultural challenges, representing JSCOE in competitions across Pune’s colleges. 2017 wasn’t just a year of accolades, but one of active engagement, skill-building, and demonstrated leadership, setting the stage for his subsequent selection as a finalist in the KPIT-Sparkle prototyping round in 2018 and his participation in the Avishkar Innovation Fair.
